concerns.note: TESTER PROMPT STALE SELECTORS (low, recurring self-improvement flag from the tester itself, runs 205+, reconfirmed run 214): the tester prompt's hardcoded Phase-3/5 diagnostic selectors (#page-total, .pdf-page-wrapper, .welcome-screen, #btn-zoom-in/out, #btn-fit-width) no longer match the live DOM (now .pdf-page[data-page-number], body.has-document, [data-panel], zoom by aria-label). The tester adapts each run and verifies the geometry that matters (containerWidth>=300, visible canvas), so verdicts are sound — BUT a future tester run that blindly trusts totalPages=0 could file a false UPLOAD-RENDER-BROKEN SYSTEM CRITICAL. Fix = update actors/tester/prompt.md canned Phase-3/5 scripts to the current selectors. Do this on a genuinely idle run (not mid-tick) and re-verify the tester still emits full Phase 1-6 + 9-step + regression after the edit (System Change Verification Protocol). Did NOT edit mid-tick (20:00 pipeline active).

STRUCTURAL SCHEDULING OVERLAP (medium): supervisor cron 15 8,20 vs pipeline 0 */4 — the 08:15/20:15 supervisor runs ALWAYS start ~15 min into the 08:00/20:00 pipeline tick and overlap its tail. Mitigation: by the security-agent stage the agents that write tasks.md are done, so tasks.md edits are safe then (only security.json live). For a guaranteed idle window move supervisor to e.g. 50 9,21 — but that's a crontab edit, route through human; do not change cron unilaterally.

Public domain cronloop.techtools.cz still resolves to the OLD VPS. Until DNS cutover: no certbot possible, all testing via http://localhost/. Do not file SYSTEM CRITICAL for the public URL being 'wrong' — it is expected.
